` element is the generic container for flow content. It has no effect on the content or layout until styled with CSS. Use semantic elements when possible.

```html ... ``` The `` element is a generic inline container for phrasing content. It does not inherently represent anything. Use it to group elements for styling or when no other semantic element is appropriate.

``` The `

` element represents a paragraph of text. Browsers automatically add margin before and after the element. It cannot contain block-level elements.
```html ... ``` The `` (anchor) element creates a hyperlink to web pages, files, email addresses, locations, or anything a URL can address. The `href` attribute specifies the destination.

```html ``` The `` element embeds an image. The `src` attribute is required. Always include `alt` for accessibility. Use `loading="lazy"` for off-screen images. Specify `width` and `height` to prevent layout shifts.

``` The `
    ` element represents an unordered list of items, typically rendered as a bulleted list. It may only contain `
  • `, `

```html
  1. Item
``` The `
    ` element represents an ordered list of items, typically rendered as a numbered list. Supports `start`, `type`, and `reversed` attributes.

  • ` element represents an item in a list. It must be contained in `

        `, or ``. In an `
          `, the `value` attribute sets the ordinal number.

```html
Header
Data
``` The `` element represents tabular data. Use ``, ``, and `` for semantic structuring. Avoid using tables for layout.
```html Cell ``` The `` element defines a row of cells in a table. It can contain `` (data cells) and `` (header cells).
```html Cell data ``` The `` element defines a standard data cell in a table. Use `colspan` and `rowspan` to span multiple columns or rows.

```html
<th scope="col" colspan="2">Header</th>
```
The `<th>` element defines a header cell in a table. Use the `scope` attribute (`col`, `row`, `colgroup`, `rowgroup`) to associate headers with cells for accessibility.

```html
<form action="/submit" method="post" enctype="multipart/form-data">
  ...
</form>
```
The `<form>` element represents a document section containing interactive controls for submitting information. Key attributes: `action`, `method`, `enctype`, `novalidate`.

```html
<input type="text" name="field" value="" placeholder="hint" required />
```
The `<input>` element creates interactive controls for web forms. The `type` attribute determines behavior: text, password, email, number, checkbox, radio, file, date, range, color, hidden, submit, etc.

```html
<button type="submit|button|reset" disabled>Label</button>
```
The `<button>` element represents a clickable button. Types: `submit` (default in forms), `button` (no default behavior), `reset` (resets form). Can contain HTML, unlike `<input type="button">`.

```html
<select name="choice" multiple size="4">
  <option value="a">A</option>
</select>
```
The `<select>` element represents a control providing a menu of options. Use `multiple` for multi-select, `size` to show more options. Group with `<optgroup>`.

```html
<option value="val" selected disabled>Label</option>
```
The `<option>` element defines an item in a `<select>`, `<optgroup>`, or `<datalist>`. The `value` attribute is sent on form submission; displayed text is the element content.

```html
<textarea name="msg" rows="4" cols="50" maxlength="500" placeholder="Type here..."></textarea>
```
The `<textarea>` element represents a multi-line plain-text editing control. Control size with `rows`/`cols` or CSS. Supports `maxlength`, `minlength`, `placeholder`, `wrap`.

```html
<label for="inputId">Label text</label>
```
The `<label>` element represents a caption for a form control. Use the `for` attribute matching the `id` of the associated control, or wrap the control inside the label.

```html

Page Title

``` The `

` element represents a top-level heading. There should be only one `

` per page. Headings help define document outline and are critical for SEO and accessibility.

```html

Section Title

``` The `

` element represents a second-level heading. Use it for major sections beneath the page title.

```html

Sub-section Title

``` The `

` element represents a third-level heading. Use it for sub-sections within an `

` section.

```html

Heading

``` The `

` element represents a fourth-level heading. Provides further sub-sectioning under `

`.

```html
Heading
``` The `
` element represents a fifth-level heading.

```html
Heading
``` The `
` element represents the lowest level of heading. Rarely needed — consider restructuring content if deeply nested.

```html

Title

...
``` The `
` element represents introductory content or a set of navigational links. It typically contains headings, logos, search forms, or navigation.

```html

© 2025

``` The `
` element represents a footer for its nearest sectioning content or sectioning root. Typically contains authorship, copyright, related links.

```html
  • Home
``` The `
` element represents a section of a page with navigation links. Not all link groups need `
` — use it for major navigation blocks. Add `aria-label` when using multiple nav elements.

```html
<main>
  <!-- primary content -->
</main>
```
The `<main>` element represents the dominant content of the `<body>`. There must be only one visible `<main>` per page. Content should be unique and not repeated across pages (skip nav, sidebars, etc.).

```html

Section Title

``` The `
` element represents a standalone section of content that doesn't have a more specific semantic element. Each section should typically have a heading.

```html

Title

Content...

``` The `
` element represents a self-contained composition intended for independent distribution or reuse — e.g., a blog post, news article, forum post, or widget.

```html

Related

Sidebar content

``` The `
` element represents content tangentially related to the content around it — sidebars, call-out boxes, related links, or pull quotes.
```html ``` The `

```html
<figcaption>Image caption</figcaption>
```
The `<figcaption>` element represents a caption or legend for a `<figure>` element. It should be the first or last child of its parent `<figure>`.

```html
Click to toggle

Hidden content revealed on toggle.

``` The `
` element creates a disclosure widget where information is visible only when toggled open. The `open` attribute controls initial state. Always include a `` child.

```html
<summary>Toggle heading</summary>
```
The `<summary>` element specifies a visible heading for a `<details>` disclosure widget. It must be the first child of `<details>`. Clicking it toggles the widget open/closed.

```html

Content

 ``` The `` element represents a dialog box or interactive component (modal or non-modal). Use `.showModal()` for modal dialogs, `.show()` for non-modal. Includes built-in focus trapping and backdrop.
```html ``` The `

```html
<slot name="header">Default content</slot>
```
The `<slot>` element is a placeholder inside a Web Component's shadow DOM. It allows consumers to insert their own markup. Named slots target specific elements with `slot="name"` attribute.

```html
<video src="movie.mp4" controls width="640" height="360" poster="thumb.jpg">
  <track kind="subtitles" src="subs.vtt" srclang="en" />
</video>
```
The `<video>` element embeds a media player for video playback. Key attributes: `controls`, `autoplay`, `muted`, `loop`, `poster`, `preload`. Use `<source>` for multiple formats and `<track>` for subtitles.

```html
<audio controls>
  <source src="audio.mp3" type="audio/mpeg" />
</audio>
```
The `<audio>` element embeds sound content. Supports `controls`, `autoplay`, `muted`, `loop`, `preload`. Use multiple `<source>` elements for format fallbacks.

```html
<canvas id="myCanvas" width="400" height="300"></canvas>
```
The `<canvas>` element is a container for graphics drawn via JavaScript (Canvas 2D API or WebGL). Set dimensions via attributes (not CSS) to avoid scaling. Falls back to inner content when unsupported.

```html
<iframe src="page.html" width="600" height="400" title="Embedded" sandbox loading="lazy"></iframe>
```
The `<iframe>` element embeds another HTML page. Use `sandbox` attribute to restrict capabilities. Use `loading="lazy"` for performance. Always include `title` for accessibility.

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />
<link rel="icon" href="favicon.ico" />
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
```
The `<link>` element specifies relationships between the document and external resources. Most commonly used for stylesheets, icons, preloading, and DNS prefetching.

```html
<meta charset="UTF-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
<meta name="description" content="Page description" />
```
The `<meta>` element provides metadata about the HTML document. Used for character encoding, viewport settings, descriptions, Open Graph tags, and HTTP equiv directives.

```html
<script src="app.js" defer></script>
<script type="module" src="mod.js"></script>
```
The `<script>` element embeds or references executable JavaScript. Use `defer` to delay execution until DOM is parsed. Use `async` for independent scripts. `type="module"` enables ES module syntax.
```html ``` The `` element contains `` elements and one `` fallback for responsive and art-directed images. The browser picks the best matching source based on media queries and supported formats.

```html
<source src="video.webm" type="video/webm" />
<source srcset="image.webp" type="image/webp" media="(min-width: 800px)" />
```
The `<source>` element specifies multiple media resources for `<picture>`, `<audio>`, or `<video>`. The browser selects the first supported source.

```html
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 100" width="100" height="100">
  <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40" fill="blue" />
</svg>
```
The `<svg>` element is a container for SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics). SVG is resolution-independent and supports shapes, paths, text, gradients, filters, and animations.

```html
Document Elements
```
Elements that define the document structure and metadata.

```html
Sectioning Elements
```
Elements that define the sections and structure of an HTML document.

```html
Text Content Elements
```
Elements used for organizing blocks of text content within the body.

```html
Inline Text Semantics
```
Elements that define the meaning, structure, or style of a word, line, or piece of text.

```html
Form Elements
```
Elements for creating interactive forms to collect user input.

```html
Media Elements
```
Elements for embedding images, video, audio, and other media content.

```html
Interactive Elements
```
Elements that provide interactive user interface components.

```html
<style>...</style>
```
Contains CSS style information for a document, or part of a document.
